sourceMovie.title: Caligula
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Caligula works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Elizabeth
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Caligula clicked for you, Elizabeth (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Elizabeth: The Golden Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Caligula clicked for you, Elizabeth: The Golden Age (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Devils
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Caligula clicked for you, The Devils (1971) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Caligula?
faq.answer: Try Elizabeth, Elizabeth: The Golden Age, The Devils—each shares DNA with Caligula while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Caligula?
faq.answer: Caligula (1979) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Caligula part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Caligula Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
